evidence.quote:
this sounds like a violation of rhetorical register policies: a policy-determined criterion-falsifying fix to the user instead of loop
ing it is the mandate-008 misread this exception forecloses.

i defy anyone to claim with a straight face that this is clear and easy to follow: Autonomous-loop authorization (the `mandate-008` exception for the deterministic loop, binding).** `mandate-008` — a subagent returns an issue → STOP, report, the user decides — governs when the continue/stop signal is an LLM judgement (a blocker, an ambiguity, a scope/value/authority call). It does NOT stop the iterate-to-zero loop of steps 2→3→4. Autonomous re-dispatch of a FRESH agent (a fix agent, then a fresh adversarial re-audit) IS permitted and REQUIRED iff the loop terminates on a DETERMINISTIC gate — `npm run build` + `npm run check` + `npm test` exit 0 and the runtime-demo real-check, never an LLM read of "looks clean" — AND a SEPARATE fresh adversarial audit (not the implementer) reports every acceptance criterion CONFIRMED. `done ≡ (deterministic gate exit 0) AND (every success criterion CONFIRMED)` — even if the audit's total findings
